homepage.text_excerpt: Munira Wilson MP - Munira.org.uk --> --> Home Help & Advice News Media Campaigns Get in touch Standing up for Twickenham I’m Munira Wilson, the Liberal Democrat MP for Twickenham. My constituency includes Twickenham, Teddington, the Hamptons, St Margarets, Strawberry Hill and Whitton. I enjoy hearing from constituents on local and national issues, so please get in touch and don’t forget to follow me on Twitter . Munira Wilson MP @MuniraWilson The Government promised to put families’ voices at the heart of special needs reform, yet reports suggest otherwise. I and Liberal Democrat MPs have written to the Education Secretary to ensure they are heard, especially on changes to children's and families' rights. Click here Join 100s of local residents Subscribe now to join Munira’s mailing list. You can choose to receive info about events, campaigns, or news, and you can unsubscribe at any time. Sign up Latest news Twickenham loses police counter – Munira says “show us the officers” Munira called for a “clear and noticeable” increase in police on the streets. Read more Munira responds to Government’s bathing water status announcement Welcomes news that Ham and Kingston is one of 13 proposed spots, but calls for Thames Water’s sewage recycling proposals to be scrapped, saying the two proposals are “fundamentally incompatible”. Read more Munira brands Government’s PFAS action plan “underwhelming” Says the UK needs urgent action, not more “dithering and delay”. Read more Munira presses the Government on deportation of Roksana Lecka Munira has been urgently seeking answers from Ministers, so that the parents and children can be reassured Lecka will not walk free in Poland. Read more Munira calls on the Met to sell Teddington Police Station for community benefit Munira speaks to the BBC outside Teddington Police Station. Read more Munira slams Government’s decision to back Heathrow’s third runway proposals Munira says the choice should not have come down to two rival bids – the choice should be for no third runway at all. Read more Munira pledges to fight to make nurseries safe for every child Munira pledged to push the Government to improve safeguarding for children in nurseries, following the horrific case of abuse at Twickenham Green Montessori, part of the Riverside Nursery group. Read more Munira challenges Twickenham police station front counter closures Munira continues to press the Government, the Metropolitan Police and the Mayor of London on these plans, including raising the issue in Parliament and in the media. Read more Munira speaks in debate on boys’ attainment and engagement in education Too many boys aren’t able to reach their potential at school, which has severe consequences for our society. Read more Munira responds to Heathrow third runway proposals With Heathrow Airport formally submitting its proposals for a third runway on 1 August 2025, Munira issued a statement opposing the plans. Read more Campaigns Protecting our precious river Munira continues to fight for Thames Water’s sewage recycling proposals at Teddington (the TDRA) to be scrapped. Read more Fighting Heathrow noise and pollution Munira is holding Heathrow and the Government to account on aircraft noise and pollution. Read more Demanding better SWR rail services Munira has consistently challenged SWR and the Government on the endless delays, cancellations and poor service that have left residents stranded. Read more Kinship carers Munira has introduced the Kinship Care Bill in Parliament to step up for those who step up for a child in crisis Read more Children’s mental health Munira is demanding urgent action to tackle the crisis in children’s mental health Read more Teddington Police Station The building should be retained for community use, not sold to the highest bidder Read more In the media Privacy Munira Wilson MP is the Member of Parliament for Twickenham. pages.text_excerpt: Campaigns - Munira.org.uk --> --> Home Help & Advice News Media Campaigns Get in touch Campaigns Munira is the Liberal Democrat Spokesperson for Education. However, her work is not confined to this area alone. Whilst she was the Health Spokesperson, she coordinated the Liberal Democrat response to the coronavirus, while continuing to fight on issues such as Heathrow expansion, local transport links and children and young people’s mental health. You can read about Munira’s work, past and present in this campaigns section. If you have a question about anything or would like to get involved, please contact Munira . Campaigns Protecting our precious river Munira continues to fight for Thames Water’s sewage recycling proposals at Teddington (the TDRA) to be scrapped. Read more Fighting Heathrow noise and pollution Munira is holding Heathrow and the Government to account on aircraft noise and pollution. Read more Demanding better SWR rail services Munira has consistently challenged SWR and the Government on the endless delays, cancellations and poor service that have left residents stranded. The number for her parliamentary office is 020 7219 3000. Alternatively, please use the contact form on this page. Munira receives a lot of correspondence, but attempts to reply in as short a time as possible. pages.title: Munira joins BBC’s Question Time & LBC’s Cross Question - Munira.org.uk
pages.h1: Munira joins BBC’s Question Time & LBC’s Cross Question
pages.text_excerpt: Munira joins BBC’s Question Time & LBC’s Cross Question - Munira.org.uk --> --> Home Help & Advice News Media Campaigns Get in touch Munira joins BBC’s Question Time & LBC’s Cross Question Published on May 2, 2024 On 25 April, Munira joined panelists on BBC’s Question Time, speaking about outrageous NHS waiting lists, the housing emergency and the Conservative Government’s shameful Rwanda policy. In response to the question, “Does Britain have a sick note culture, with a lazy workforce?” Munira said: “Given the cost of living crisis, where people can, they’re working not just one job, but sometimes two or three jobs, so that they don’t have to choose between heating and eating. And for those who can’t work, they desperately want to work.” Watch the clip here . Watch the full episode on BBC iPlayer here . On 22 April, Munira participated in LBC’s Cross Question panel hosted by Iain Dale and called for the Government to use their existing powers to ensure that water companies are held to account. “As a London MP, my constituents would have to pay these ridiculous water bills that they’re proposing. I think it’s utterly outrageous and my constituents and the millions of Thames Water customers shouldn’t have to foot the bill for their failures,” said Munira. Watch the full interview here . pages.title: Munira speaks to BBC Radio London about Thames Water's Teddington proposals - Munira.org.uk
pages.h1: Munira speaks to BBC Radio London about Thames Water’s Teddington proposals
pages.text_excerpt: Munira speaks to BBC Radio London about Thames Water's Teddington proposals - Munira.org.uk --> --> Home Help & Advice News Media Campaigns Get in touch Munira speaks to BBC Radio London about Thames Water’s Teddington proposals Published on September 21, 2023 On Wednesday 6 September, Munira led a debate on Thames Water’s controversial plans to pump treated sewage into the river upstream of Teddington Lock. With local residents rightly outraged by the proposals, Munira spoke to various media outlets about her debate in Parliament, and why she believes the proposals should be scrapped. Read the article in the Evening Standard here: Water chiefs condemned over ‘scandal’ of sewage in Thames . In a call-in to LBC before the debate, Munira highlighted the strength of local feeling, and why the scheme just doesn’t add up. Munira’s debate came at a time when sewage scandals continue to dominate the news. The Government came under fire for trying to scrap EU pollution laws (a move Liberal Democrats helped block) and for accusations it may have broken the law over how it regulates sewage releases. Meanwhile, water companies continue to breach sewage regulations whilst losing millions of litres of water to leaks every day. In an interview with BBC Radio London, Munira raised the question of trust in water companies. The Liberal Democrats have been leading a national campaign on the water industry – with a special focus on the scandals around sewage and water quality. Munira raised the issue of proper scrutiny and regulation. Read more on Munira’s campaign page here . Next > Join 100s of local residents Subscribe now to join Munira’s mailing list.
